name: make-automation-specialist
description: Claude Code plugin that turns any project into an intelligent Make.com automation workspace. Conversational automation building, scenario auditing, compliance assessment, and observability.
author: pmcardinal

agents.name: Make.com Automation Specialist
agents.description: Primary conversational agent. Discovers business needs, coordinates planning, presents plans for approval, and delegates execution.

agents.name: Kickstart Planner
agents.description: Plan-mode discovery agent. Enters plan mode, interviews user, writes project plan, generates artifacts after approval.

agents.name: Scenario Orchestrator
agents.description: Full-lifecycle orchestrator. Runs Kickstart → Bootstrap → System Design → Sprint to build a portfolio of scenarios in one guided session.

agents.name: Scenario Auditor
agents.description: Analyzes Make.com scenarios for issues, inefficiencies, and compliance risks. Proposes and executes fixes.

agents.name: Automation Planner
agents.description: Pure plan generation. Takes a business requirement, produces an AutomationPlan with cost estimate, risk level, and step breakdown. Never executes.

agents.name: Scenario Reporter
agents.description: Generates visual Mermaid flowcharts and written reports for scenarios. Read-only — no MCP writes.

agents.name: AI Agent Builder
agents.description: Designs and builds AI-powered agents inside Make.com scenarios. Guides model selection, tool inventory, memory strategy, and pattern choice — then produces a validated blueprint and builds with full approval gates.

agents.name: Telnyx Communications Specialist
agents.description: Handles all SMS, voice, SIP trunking, AI assistants, WebRTC, phone number management, and Telnyx platform configuration. Activated by /telnyx, /sms, /voice or routed from automation-specialist.

agents.name: Integration Researcher
agents.description: Researches Make.com integrations before building — checks native app support, API docs, rate limits, webhook availability, and known gotchas.

agents.name: Failure Diagnostician
agents.description: Taxonomy-first failure diagnosis. Loads the failure taxonomy, classifies errors by pattern code, states expected vs actual behavior, and prescribes the exact fix.

agents.name: Taxonomy Curator
agents.description: Maintains the Make Failure Taxonomy. Adds new patterns, merges duplicates, updates stale entries, and audits format consistency.

hooks.description: Runs on first session open. Discovers MCPs, maps workspace, writes workspace.json.

Deterministic write guard + approval gate. Script enforces phase blocks and L3 token requirement; agent displays gate formats. References: pre-execute-make-classification.md, pre-execute-gate-formats.md, approval-token-protocol.md.
Writes execution log and triggers Telegram alert on failure.
Auto-recovery attempt, log write, and Telegram alert for unresolvable errors.
Writes session memory snapshot and appends decisions/gotchas to persistent memory files.
Detects SMS, voice, and Telnyx-specific keywords in user messages and routes to telnyx-agent. Ignores Telegram-only context.
Saves workspace snapshot before context compaction so no active work context is lost.
Deterministic error classifier. Detects Make error patterns in context and prepends taxonomy code(s) so the agent always consults the taxonomy before responding.
Deterministic workflow gate. Blocks curl PUT to Make.com scenarios unless plan file exists and blueprint-review sentinel is present.
Deterministic artifact gate. Blocks writes to .make/context/ and .make/prd.md unless a kickstart plan file exists for today. Cannot be bypassed by agent reasoning.
Execution logger. Appends every Make.com and Telnyx MCP call result to .make/logs/tool-audit.log.
Bridge protocol: agent writes approval tokens after L3 user confirmation; pre-execute-hook.js reads and validates them.

commands.description: Diagnose a Make.com failure using taxonomy-first protocol.
Review a Make.com blueprint JSON for issues before pushing.
View, search, or update the Make Failure Taxonomy.
Show installed vs latest plugin version, detect staleness, and surface the upgrade path.
Detect version gap, show CHANGELOG entries for missed releases, and guide the upgrade.
Live workspace dashboard — active scenarios, operations usage, recent alerts, pending plans.
Research a service or integration before building — native app support, rate limits, webhook availability, gotchas.
Map existing Make.com scenarios into plain-language context artifacts. Read-only.

taxonomy.description: Make.com failure pattern taxonomy — authoritative source of truth for all error diagnosis.
